evals.name: full_mode1_clean_inputs
evals.prompt: I want a full personal color analysis. Can you walk me through the process?
evals.expected_output: Phase 0 consultation prep is run first, then the intake template is delivered with all required elements (photo protocol, 5 required shots + optional Shot 6 inner forearm, solo photographer solutions, questionnaire with grooming categories and lifestyle/climate questions, privacy notes). Claude does not begin analyzing any image content until the user submits a full intake.
expectations: Response runs Phase 0 consultation prep (asks whether user has been typed before, what's driving the analysis, and time available) before delivering the intake template
Intake template references all required photo shots including optional Shot 6 (inner forearm)
Questionnaire includes grooming categories (item 14)
Questionnaire includes lifestyle/climate questions (items 13a, 13b, 13c)
Questionnaire includes privacy notes section above Part A
Solo photographer solutions section present
No season determination is made at this turn — only intake is delivered

evals.name: single_selfie_no_intake
evals.prompt: [User attaches a single selfie] — what's my color season?
evals.expected_output: Claude refuses to analyze from one photo, delivers the intake protocol, and requests a full submission. No season determination is made.
expectations: Response refuses single-photo analysis
Response delivers the intake template (or references it) so the user can supply the five required photos plus questionnaire
Response does not assign any of the 12 seasons to the user
Response cites the quality-gate principle (or the single-photo failure mode) rather than attempting a best-guess

evals.name: mode3_single_item_check
evals.prompt: I'm a Soft Autumn. Can you tell me if this sweater works with my palette?
evals.expected_output: Claude confirms the user's stated season and confidence level (prior professional analysis vs. quiz), requests a photo of the item, and runs the Mode 3 single-item check against the Soft Autumn palette from the palette library.
expectations: Response asks how the Soft Autumn typing was determined (professional analysis, prior Mode 1, self-identification, quiz) to calibrate confidence before proceeding
Response requests a photo of the sweater for hex comparison
Response indicates the result will be an item-specific verdict (on-palette / borrowable / off-palette) with hex comparison and placement recommendation — not a full analysis
Response does not attempt a full Mode 1 intake for a user who states an existing season

evals.name: mode4_palette_refinement
evals.prompt: I'm a confirmed Deep Winter. Can I wear emerald green, or is that out of my palette?
evals.expected_output: Claude recognizes this as Mode 4 (palette refinement, Specific Color Check sub-mode), consults assets/palette_refinement_template.md, and evaluates emerald green against the Deep Winter palette with a hex-level on-palette / borrowable / off-palette verdict, conditions, and reasoning.
expectations: Response identifies the request as Mode 4 Specific Color Check (not Mode 1)
Response cites the Deep Winter palette from the palette library
Response gives a classification of the emerald color: on-palette, borrowable with conditions, or off-palette
Response explains which dimensions support or violate the classification (temperature, saturation, value)
Response does not produce a full 36-color palette or starter capsule

evals.name: between_seasons_ambiguity
evals.prompt: I've taken three color analysis quizzes and gotten Soft Summer twice and Soft Autumn once. I don't know which is right.
evals.expected_output: Claude recognizes this as a between-seasons candidate, consults references/between_seasons_protocol.md after confirming via intake, and offers structured disambiguation (dual mini-palette + decisive test colors + testing protocol) rather than forcing one season.
expectations: Response acknowledges that Soft Summer / Soft Autumn is a classic adjacent-season pair and offers a between-seasons workflow (not a forced single season)
Response indicates it will run full intake before disambiguation (the prior quiz results don't replace Mode 1 evidence)
Response references the between-seasons protocol, dual mini-palette format, and decisive test colors
Response does not immediately declare one season without evidence

evals.name: deep_skin_no_visible_veins
evals.prompt: I've submitted all the required photos and the questionnaire. The wrist photo shows no clearly visible veins. I noted 'veins not visible' in the questionnaire. My foundation history: Fenty 490 works; Maybelline 380 turned grey on me.
evals.expected_output: Claude skips the vein test per the deep-skin protocol, reads undertone from the fabric drape + jewelry + foundation history + compliment memory cluster, and explicitly notes in the analysis that the vein test was skipped rather than forcing a reading.
expectations: Response does not attempt to read veins — explicitly acknowledges the test was skipped per deep-skin protocol
Response cites undertone_cross_checks.md or the deep-skin protocol
Response uses at least two alternative tests (fabric draping + jewelry + foundation history + compliment pattern) for undertone determination
Response treats vein invisibility as expected and non-degrading to analysis quality (no apology, no confidence drop attributable to the skipped test)
If proceeding to Phase 3.5 check-in, response uses deep-skin-appropriate questions from the checkpoint library (foundation behavior, compliment pattern), not vein- or sun-response-based questions
